response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine breadth across distributed systems, AI-agent infrastructure, and developer tooling — his current role at re:cinq directly involves MCP code generators and agentic workflows, which aligns well with the AI-native environment described. He has demonstrated high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second and carries a credible staff-level track record. However, Python — listed as a must-have — is entirely absent from his resume and skill set, which is a meaningful gap for this role. His stakeholder management and operational ownership evidence is thin and largely implied rather than demonstrated explicitly.
strengths: Current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq is a direct match for the AI-native engineering environment
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O)
Strong TypeScript and Node.js across multiple production roles, complemented by deep polyglot breadth (DLang, C++, Swift, Java)
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list despite being a stated must-have for the role
No explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, incident response practices, or on-call operational ownership
Distributed systems depth is real but the most relevant experience (Dunnhumby) is now 5+ years old; current role scope is less clearly large-scale
